title: Psephellus hadimensis
intro: Psephellus hadimensis, also known as Turkish wormwood, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Psephellus hadimensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, and is also used in the production of essential oils and capsules for various health remedies.
taxonomy: Psephellus hadimensis is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The genus Psephellus is a group of plants within the subfamily Carduoideae, which is a part of the larger family Asteraceae. The classification of Psephellus hadimensis is further detailed as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Subfamily: Carduoideae, Tribe: Cardueae, Genus: Psephellus. The classification places Psephellus hadimensis within the family of daisies. This classification reflects the plant's botanical characteristics and evolutionary history.
